channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Iran's supreme leader rejects nuclear talks with US after Trump's overtures
item.description: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Iran's supreme leader, said in a series of X posts Saturday he would not negotiate a nuclear deal with the U.S. due to its demands.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: International Women's Day protests demand equal rights and an end to discrimination, sexual violence
item.description: ISTANBUL (AP) — Women took to the streets of cities across Europe, Africa, South America and elsewhere to mark International Women’s Day with demands for ending inequality and gender-based violence.
item.dc:creator: MEHMET GUZEL and ANDREW WILKS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea's impeached President Yoon released from prison
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s impeached conservative President Yoon Suk Yeol was released from prison on Saturday, a day after a Seoul court canceled his arrest to allow him to stand trial for rebellion without being detained.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 2 days of clashes and revenge killings in Syria leave more than 1,000 people dead
item.description: A war monitoring group says the death toll from two days of clashes between Syrian security forces and loyalists of ousted President Bashar Assad and revenge killings that followed has risen to more than 1,000, including nearly 750 civilians, making it one of the deadliest outbreaks of violence sinc
item.dc:creator: Bassem Mroue And Sarah El Deeb, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Bipartisan legislation seeks to rein in Erdogan's Turkey over ties to US foes
item.description: A bipartisan group of congressmen have taken issue with NATO ally Turkey aligning its foreign policy interests against the U.S. and its Western allies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN draft report on children in conflict zones raises eyebrows with framing of Israelis
item.description: The U.N.'s annual report on children in conflict zones, which is set to be published in June, is already getting attention for its framing of Israeli victims and for omitting Hamas crimes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Shooting in Canada leaves a dozen wounded, police say
item.description: A shooting Friday evening at a pub in Toronto, Canada, left a dozen people wounded, as police continue to search for the suspect, according to officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rare cyclone weakens to a tropical low weather system as it approaches the Australian east coast
item.description: BRISBANE, Australia (AP) — A tropical cyclone weakened to a tropical low weather system on Saturday as it approached Brisbane, Australia’s third-most populous city, bringing flooding rain that was expected to lash the coastal region for days.
item.dc:creator: JOHN PYE and ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Russia attacks Ukraine's energy supplies as US cuts its access to satellite images
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia attacked Ukrainian energy facilities with dozens of missiles and drones during the night, officials said Friday, hobbling the country’s ability to deliver heat and light to its citizens and to power weapons factories vital to its defenses.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Court orders South Korean President Yoon freed from jail for martial law trial
item.description: South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol was ordered to be released from jail by a South Korean court after the legal period for his formal arrest expired before his indictment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump seeking new nuclear deal with Iran with letter to supreme leader
item.description: President Trump said Thursday he has written a letter to Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, calling on the country to negotiate a new nuclear deal with the United States.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, The Hill

item.title: Canadian foreign minister: Trump tariffs 'pretext' to annexation
item.description: Canadian Foreign Minister Mélanie Joly on Thursday claimed that President Trump's tariffs against Canada — the U.S.'s largest trading partner — are just a "pretext" for the administration's wish to eventually annex the nation.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Huge unexploded World War II-era bomb found in crowded Paris residential area
item.description: A half-ton World War II-era bomb found by workers near train tracks outside of Paris is disrupting train and road travel across the region.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Police in Bangladesh use batons and tear gas to disperse rally by banned Islamist group
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — Police used batons and stun grenades on Friday to disperse thousands of members of the banned Hizbut Tahrir group as they marched near Bangladesh’s main Baitul Mokarram Mosque in the country’s capital.
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN says 186 missing and at least 2 dead after migrant boats capsize off Yemen and Djibouti
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — At least two people have died and 186 others are missing after four boats carrying migrants from Africa capsized overnight in waters off Yemen and Djibouti, the U.N.'s migration agency said on Friday.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Boy with loaded shotgun boarded plane in Australia, tackled by passenger
item.description: A 17-year-old boy with a loaded shotgun boarded a plane at an Australian airport before being restrained by a pilot and two passengers, police said Friday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: EU leaders commit to working together after Trump signals that Europe must defend itself
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — European Union leaders on Thursday committed to working together to bolster the continent's defenses and to free up hundreds of billions of euros for security after U.S. President Donald Trump’s repeated warnings that he would cut them adrift to face the threat of Russia alone.
item.dc:creator: RAF CASERT and LORNE COOK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump NATO stance shows 'simple-minded view of the world': Bolton
item.description: Trump said the U.S. may not help defend NATO members that fail to meet their military spending commitment.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Trump envoy says Ukrainians 'brought it on themselves' after US pauses aid and intelligence sharing
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Ukraine was given “fair warning” by the White House before President Donald Trump this week ordered a pause on U.S. military assistance and intelligence sharing with Kyiv, a senior administration official said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Hamas brushes off Trump's threat and says it will only free hostages in return for a lasting truce
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Hamas on Thursday brushed off President Donald Trump's latest threat and reiterated that it will only free the remaining Israeli hostages in exchange for a lasting ceasefire in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Mother of Abbey Gate victim says Biden ‘robbed’ family of justice
item.description: "If we can take one terrorist off the list of harming Americans, especially our soldiers, then that is a good day," Paula Knauss Selph told NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas disregards Trump’s ‘last warning’ over hostage release
item.description: Hamas' response is an apparent dismissal of President Donald Trump's "last warning" issued on social media.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Pope rests after peaceful night during third week of pneumonia treatment
item.description: Pope Francis was resting Thursday after a peaceful night during his third week of hospital treatment for double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Schools closed, public transport halted as rare cyclone approaches Australia
item.description: Tropical Cyclone Alfred is forecast to cross the Queensland state coast somewhere between the Sunshine Coast region and the city of Gold Coast to the south early Saturday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN PYE and ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Kabul airport bombing suspect 'will die in prison': Sebastian Gorka
item.description: Sharifullah is charged in federal court in Virginia with providing material support to a foreign terrorist organization.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Vargas, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration pauses flow of intelligence to Ukraine that helps on battlefield
item.description: CIA Director John Ratcliffe called the suspension a “pause” and said it came after the disastrous meeting between Trump and Zelenskyy in the Oval Office last week. Ratcliffe said Trump wanted to know that Zelenskyy was serious about peace.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Kremlin says a 2022 Ukrainian decree bans Zelenskyy from talks with Putin
item.description: Russia on Wednesday asked how Ukraine could attend potential talks on ending their three-year war when a Ukrainian decree from 2022 rules out negotiations with President Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel’s cutoff of supplies to Gaza sends prices soaring as aid stockpiles dwindle
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel’s cutoff of food, fuel, medicine and other supplies to Gaza’s 2 million people has sent prices soaring and humanitarian groups into overdrive trying to distribute dwindling stocks to the most vulnerable.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: What Trump’s aid freeze means for Ukraine war
item.description: President Donald Trump’s freeze on U.S. aid going to Ukraine threatens to create severe problems for Kyiv in its war against Russia, leaving the country more vulnerable to aerial attacks on population centers and key infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, WGN 9

item.title: 12 killed, 30 wounded in Pakistan military base attack launched by suicide bombers
item.description: PESHAWAR, Pakistan (AP) — Two suicide bombings breached a wall at a military base in northwestern Pakistan before other attackers stormed the compound and were repelled in violence that killed at least 12 people and wounded 30 others, according to officials and a local hospital.
item.dc:creator: RIAZ KHAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: James Harrison, whose blood saved 2.4M babies dies, at 88
item.description: An Australian man credited with saving 2.4 million babies through his record-breaking blood plasma donations over six decades, has died at 88, his family said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Arab countries are working on a postwar plan for Gaza
item.description: Arab foreign ministers met in Cairo on Monday for talks focusing on Egypt's plan to rebuild the war-ravaged Gaza Strip, hoping to counter President Donald Trump’s proposal to expel Palestinians from the coastal enclave and take it over.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Trump and his allies lash out at Ukraine’s Zelenskyy and suggest he may need to resign
item.description: President Donald Trump ’s senior aides and allies lashed out at Ukrainian leader Volodymyr Zelenskyy from Washington as he attended a European summit in London to rally international support for his military’s fight against the Russian invasion.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pope Francis suffers new breathing crises, is back on noninvasive ventilation: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis suffered two new episodes of acute respiratory crises Monday and was put back on noninvasive mechanical ventilation, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Francis off mechanical ventilation entirely, signaling he's overcome risks from respiratory crisis
item.description: Pope Francis said he was living his hospitalization as an experience of profound solidarity with people who are sick and suffering everywhere.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and PAOLO SANTALUCIA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel cuts off Gaza aid to pressure Hamas to accept a new ceasefire proposal
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel stopped the entry of all goods and supplies into the Gaza Strip on Sunday and warned of “additional consequences” if Hamas doesn’t accept a new proposal to extend a fragile ceasefire, while key mediator Egypt accused Israel of using “starvation as a weapon.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: UK, France and Ukraine agree to work on ceasefire plan for Russia's war in Ukraine
item.description: Britain, France and Ukraine have agreed to work on a ceasefire plan to present to the United States, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Sunday as European leaders assembled in London to discuss ending the war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
